title: Hotel Check-in in China
metaDescription: Essential tips for foreign tourists checking into hotels in China, including passport requirements, approved hotels, and payment advice.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.content: Only hotels licensed to host foreign guests (涉外酒店) can check you in — always confirm this before booking. Your passport is mandatory and will be scanned by the hotel, as they are required to register foreign visitors with local police. Check-in usually begins at 2:00 PM, but you can request early check-in depending on availability.

sections.heading: Key Tips
sections.content: Keep your passport handy at all times — you cannot check in without it, and a photocopy will not be accepted. Download a translation app or have your hotel details written in Chinese, as many front-desk staff at smaller hotels may not speak English. Carry some cash or have Alipay/WeChat Pay set up, since not all hotels accept foreign credit cards.

sections.heading: What to Remember
sections.content: Expect to pay a refundable security deposit, usually by cash or card hold. Some booking platforms may list hotels that no longer accept foreigners, so double-check directly with the hotel before arrival. Always keep your hotel's business card with the address in Chinese — it's invaluable for taxi drivers when you need to find your way back.

keyTakeaways: Always confirm the hotel is licensed to accept foreign guests before booking
Your physical passport is required for check-in — copies are not accepted
Carry cash or set up Alipay/WeChat Pay as backup payment options
